questions.question: According to the restorative theory of sleep, what is one of the primary functions of sleep?

options.text: To conserve energy by reducing metabolic activity to near zero
To allow the body and brain to repair themselves and consolidate memories
To practice survival skills through dream rehearsal
To reduce consciousness so the brain can shut down completely

questions.explanation: The restorative theory proposes that sleep allows the body to repair cellular damage, release growth hormone, strengthen the immune system, and consolidate memories. The brain also clears metabolic waste products through the glymphatic system during sleep.

questions.question: Which of the following is a documented consequence of chronic sleep deprivation?

options.text: Increased emotional stability and reduced stress
Impaired attention, weakened immune function, and increased risk of depression

questions.explanation: Chronic sleep deprivation impairs cognitive functions like attention and memory, weakens the immune system, increases emotional instability, and raises the risk of depression, obesity, and cardiovascular disease. Sleep debt accumulates and is difficult to fully repay.
